Big news: after we demanded they stand down, the Trump admin stopped its plans to rip out 80% of our nation's ocean research equipment.

This proposal should never have seen the light of day. Our coastal economies deserve better than being treated as collateral damage in an

We have a chance to build cheap, clean, reliable power right off our own coasts. 

Instead, Trump is using your tax dollars to kill these projects and make America more dependent on dirty, volatile fossil fuels.

Trump spent $14 million taxpayer dollars to turn the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool into an algae pond.  

He’s flushing your money down the toilet for his harebrained projects.

Our National Parks exist to tell the American story, the full truth – not a politically sanitized version of it.

I'm glad the courts have finally stepped in to stop Trump's attempts to whitewash American history and deny science.

The Trump admin's effort to dismantle our nation's ocean observing system is illegal, wasteful, and puts all of us at risk. 

@sciencedems and I are demanding they reverse this immediately.

assessment.summary: The captured X archive has 4 Kurdistan Lens tweet hits for Jared Huffman. The social-media posture reads as critical or hostile-language signal because the strongest matched frame is critical language that needs source-level review. Top matched terms: Oil / Energy.
assessment.whatItWasLike: When this account mentioned the Kurdish file, the language most often clustered around critical language that needs source-level review. I count 0 direct Kurdistan/Kurdish-region term hits, 0 security-frame hits, 0 supportive or diplomatic hits, 0 humanitarian/minority hits, and 4 energy/economic hits in the exported profile window. The latest matched tweet is dated 2026-06-10T19:51:36Z and should be opened before treating this as a final stance.

California’s election is Tuesday, but you don’t have to wait until election day to vote. You can vote early at a local voting site or return your ballot to a secure ballot drop box.

Not sure if you’re registered to vote in Tuesday’s election? You can check your status at https://t.co/iI5owVks6g. 

If you aren't yet, no worries – same-day registration will be available at polling locations beginning at 7am until 8pm on June 2.

California’s Primary Election is next Tuesday – now's the time to make your plan to submit your ballot at a drop box, visit an early voting site, or vote on election day. Information on your voting options can be found here at the CA Secretary of State’s website:
